commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=28553e162c3f36511c2ac63b7afe483dfd939dc9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces MiniMax VLM successful-response `res.json()` parsing with the shared bounded provider JSON reader and adds oversized-response cancellation tests.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +80. Total +88 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main still uses an unbounded `res.json()` success-path read in `minimaxUnderstandImage`, and the PR head adds a loopback test for the oversized-response path, though I did not run it because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer compatibility acceptance remain required, and the lint fixes are mechanical but do not remove the external real-behavior proof gate.

target.title: fix(agents): bound MiniMax VLM and native PDF provider JSON response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`minimaxUnderstandImage` calls `await res.json()` on the MiniMax VLM API response without a read limit. A large or malicious response could buffer arbitrarily large payloads before parsing, risking OOM.

The native PDF provider paths were already bounded in #97872 — this PR now only covers the remaining MiniMax VLM path.

## Why This Change Was Made

All provider response reads should use `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap) to prevent unbounded memory consumption. Trace-Id diagnostics are preserved through the error chain so failed calls remain debuggable.

## User Impact

MiniMax VLM image understanding calls are now protected against oversized JSON responses. Normal responses work identically. Oversized responses fail with a clear error message including the Trace-Id for debugging.

## Evidence

**Environment**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 6cb82eaab8

**14/14 tests pass**, including **real HTTP server loopback proof**:
```
 ✓ rejects oversized VLM response bodies, preserves Trace-Id, and cancels the stream
 ✓ rejects oversized VLM responses from a real HTTP server (behavior proof)
 ✓ bounds large provider error response bodies
```

**Real HTTP server proof** (`minimax-vlm.normalizes-api-key.test.ts`):
```ts
// Creates a real http.createServer that writes an oversized JSON stream.
// The bounded reader cancels the socket mid-flight — proves the cap is
// enforced against a real TCP connection, not just a mock.
const server = http.createServer((_req, res) => {
  res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "application/json" });
  // writes 4 MiB chunks in a loop until socket is destroyed
});
// → throws "MiniMax VLM: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
// → socket destroyed before full body buffered
```

**All existing provider tests pass with no regression**:
```
$ pnpm test src/agents/minimax-vlm.normalizes-api-key.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
```

## Risk

Low. `readProviderJsonResponse` is the same 16 MiB capped helper used across all provider JSON reads. Native PDF paths already use it (#97872). Trace-Id is preserved in error messages for debuggability. No config, auth, or API surface changes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: cxbasdev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
